description_long: This object is a partially peeled orange, revealing the segmented, pale orange flesh beneath its textured, dimpled outer rind. The peel is curled away in a spiral, exposing about half of the fruit and creating a dynamic, naturalistic appearance. The rind is a medium orange color with a slightly rough, matte finish, while the inner flesh is lighter and appears moist and fibrous. The spiral of the peel hangs loosely, suggesting the orange has just been peeled by hand. The overall shape is round, with the exposed segments showing the characteristic radial pattern of a citrus fruit. This object would commonly be found discarded or dropped in urban environments such as sidewalks, parks, or near public seating areas.
description: A partially peeled orange with a spiral of rind hanging off, exposing the segmented, pale orange flesh beneath the textured outer skin.
description_view_0: The orange is seen from above, highlighting the exposed segments and the spiral peel curling away from the fruit.
description_view_1: A side view showing the transition from the intact rind to the peeled section, with the spiral peel extending outward.
description_view_2: The view focuses on the unpeeled, intact side of the orange, with the spiral peel visible at the edge.
description_view_3: The orange is mostly seen from the back, showing the unpeeled rind and the start of the spiral peel curling away.
attribute_location_or_placement: sidewalk, park bench, street corner, public trash bin
